preamble: The shift from the near-open front vowel [æ] to the back/central unrounded vowel [ʌ] (the caret or 'strut' vowel) is a process of backing and lowering. It is a key component of modern dialectal vowel shifts in North America.

examples.note: As part of the California Vowel Shift, the TRAP vowel retracts toward the LOT/STRUT space in non-nasal environments.
